comment_text.sn2.22:1.1: Khema is a common name meaning “safety, blessedness, sanctuary”.
comment_text.sn2.22:4.4: I follow Bodhi in finishing the godling’s verses at this point.
comment_text.sn2.22:7.3: “Jaws of death” (maccumukha) is also at Snp 4.2:5.3. Compare Kaṭha Upaniṣad 2.1.1, “he escapes from the jaws of death” (tanmṛtyumukhātpramucyate).
comment_text.sn2.22:7.4: See Bodhi, Connected Discourses note 174 for readings in these verses. | The final line here and in the previous verse are identical in Pali (akkhacchinnova jhāyati) but require different senses. The commentary reads the first as akkhacchinno avajhāyati (“frets with broken axle”) and the second akkhacchinno iva jhāyati (“frets like one whose axle is broken”). The prefixed verb is not necessary for the sense (cp. Snp 4.7:5.2, Dhp 155:3), so instead I take the first line as akkhacchinno eva jhāyati (“frets as soon as their axle is broken”).

root_text.sn2.22:1.1: Ekamantaṁ ṭhito kho khemo devaputto bhagavato santike imā gāthāyo abhāsi:

translation_text.sn2.22:1.1: Standing to one side, the godling Khema recited these verses in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn2.22:3.1: It’s not good to do a deed
translation_text.sn2.22:3.3: for which you weep and wail,
translation_text.sn2.22:3.4: as its effect stays with you.
translation_text.sn2.22:4.1: It is good to do a deed
translation_text.sn2.22:4.2: that doesn’t plague you later on,
translation_text.sn2.22:4.4: as its effect stays with you.”
translation_text.sn2.22:5.2: what you know is for your own welfare.
translation_text.sn2.22:5.3: A thinker, a wise one would not proceed
translation_text.sn2.22:6.1: Suppose a cart driver leaves the highway,
translation_text.sn2.22:6.3: They enter upon a rough road,
translation_text.sn2.22:6.4: and fret as soon as their axle is broken.
translation_text.sn2.22:7.1: So too, a dullard leaves the good
translation_text.sn2.22:7.2: to follow what’s against the good.
translation_text.sn2.22:7.3: Fallen in the jaws of death,
translation_text.sn2.22:7.4: they fret like one whose axle is broken.”

variant_text.sn2.22:6.1: maṭṭhaṁ → panthaṁ (bj, pts1ed, pts2ed); pasatthaṁ (sya-all, km)
